segments.text: Good morning.
segments.translation: 早上好。

segments.text: In the last few lectures I've been talking about the history of domestic building construction.
segments.translation: 在前几次课里，我一直在谈论国内建筑构造的历史。

segments.text: But today I want to begin looking at some contemporary, experimental designs for housing.
segments.translation: 但今天，我想开始看一些对于住宅的现代、实验性的设计。

segments.text: So, I'm going to start with a house which is constructed more or less under the ground.
segments.translation: 因此，我会从一个或多或少建在地底的房子开始。

segments.text: And one of the interesting things about this project is that the owners - both professionals but not architects - wanted to be closely involved, so they decided to manage the project themselves.
segments.translation: 这个项目趣事之一是业主们希望密切参与，他们是从业者但非设计师，于是他们决定自己管理该项目。

segments.text: Their chief aim was to create somewhere that was as environmentally-friendly as possible.
segments.translation: 他们的主要目的是要创造出尽可能环保的地方。

segments.text: But at the same time they wanted to live somewhere peaceful - they'd both grown up in a rural area and disliked urban life.
segments.translation: 但同时，他们也想要生活在一个平和的地方，他们都在乡村的地方成长，也不喜欢城市生活。

segments.text: So the first thing they did was to look for a site.
segments.translation: 所以他们做的第一件事是找个地方。

segments.text: And they found a disused stone quarry in a beautiful area.
segments.translation: 他们在一个美丽的地方找到一处废弃的采石场。

segments.text: The price was relatively low, and they liked the idea of recycling the land, as it were.
segments.translation: 价格相对低廉，而且他们喜欢以土地原有的形式再次利用的想法。

segments.text: As it was, the quarry was an ugly blot on the landscape, and it wasn't productive any longer, either.
segments.translation: 事实上，采石场是风景里一个丑陋的污点，它也不再有任何生产效率。

segments.text: They consulted various architects and looked at a number of designs before finally deciding on one.
segments.translation: 他们咨询了各种建筑师，在最终选择一个设计方案前看了很多。

segments.text: As I've said, it was a design for a sort of underground house, and it was built into the earth itself, with two storeys.
segments.translation: 正如我所说的，它是一种地下房屋的设计，它被建在地里，有两层。

segments.text: The north, east and west sides were set in the earth, and only the sloping, south-facing side was exposed to light.
segments.translation: 北边、东边和西边都建在地里，只有斜边和朝南的面光。

segments.text: That was made of a double layer of very strong glass.
segments.translation: 那是用高强度的双层玻璃做的。

segments.text: There were also photovoltaic tiles fixed to the top and bottom of this sloping wall.
segments.translation: 在斜边墙的顶部和底部还都固定了光伏砖。

segments.text: These are tiles that are designed to store energy from the sun.
segments.translation: 这些都是设计用来从日光中存储能量的砖。

segments.text: And the walls had a layer of foam around them too, to increase the insulation.
segments.translation: 这些墙面也有一层泡沫环绕，以加强保温效果。

segments.text: Now, what is of interest to us about this project is the features which make the building energy-efficient.
segments.translation: 现在，我们对这个项目感兴趣的是使建筑节能的特点。

segments.text: Sunlight floods in through the glass wall, and to maximise it there are lots of mirrors and windows inside the house.
segments.translation: 阳光透过玻璃墙进来，并且为使它最大限度地增强，房屋里有很多镜子和窗户。

segments.text: That helps to spread the light around.
segments.translation: 这帮助光线散播。

segments.text: So that's the first thing - light is utilised as fully as possible.
segments.translation: 所以，这是第一件事–光线被最大程度利用。

segments.text: In addition, the special tiles on the outside convert energy from the sun and generate some of the house's electricity.
segments.translation: 此外，外部的特殊砖面转化太阳能，并发电供给一部分房屋用电。

segments.text: In fact, and it's possible that in future the house may even generate an electricity surplus, and that the owners will be able to sell some to the national grid.
segments.translation: 事实上，在未来的房子可能会产生电盈余，业主可能会把一部分卖给国家电网。

segments.text: As well as that, wherever possible, recycled materials have been used.
segments.translation: 同样，只要可能，循环使用的材料都被利用了。

segments.text: For example, the floors are made of reclaimed wood.
segments.translation: 比如，地板是再生木材所造。

segments.text: And the owners haven't bought a single item of new furniture - they just kept what they already had.
segments.translation: 并且，业主也没有购买任何一件新的家具，他们只保留了他们所有的。

segments.text: And then there's the system for dealing with the waste produced in the house.
segments.translation: 然后，房屋里有一个处理产生的垃圾的系统。

segments.text: This is dealt with organically - it's purified by being filtered through reed beds which have been planted for that purpose in the garden.
segments.translation: 这是被有机处理的，它通过芦苇地进行过滤净化，这些芦苇地是为此目的被种植在花园里。

segments.text: So the occupants of the house won't pollute the land or use any damaging chemicals.
segments.translation: 因此，房子的居住者不会污染土地，也不会使用任何有害的化学物质。

segments.text: It's true that the actual construction of the house was harmful to the environment,mainly because they had to use massive amounts of concrete - one of the biggest sources of carbon dioxide in manufacturing.
segments.translation: 房子的实际建筑是对环境有害的，这是事实，主要原因是需要用到大量水泥，制造业中最大的二氧化碳来源之一。

segments.text: And, as you know, this is very damaging to the environment.
segments.translation: 并且，你们知道的，这是对环境特别有害的。

segments.text: In total, the house construction has released 70 tons of carbon dioxide into the air.
segments.translation: 总的说，房屋的建设已经向大气释放了70吨的二氧化碳。

segments.text: Now that's a frightening thought.
segments.translation: 现在这是个可怕的想法。

segments.text: However, once the initial 'debt' has been cleared - and it's been calculated that this will only take fifteen years - this underground house won't cost anything - environmentally I mean-because unlike ordinary houses, it is run in a way that is completely environmentally friendly.
segments.translation: 然而，一旦最初的“债务”已被清除，根据计算这将只需要15年，这座地下屋将不会花费任何东西，我说的是环境方面，因为和普通房屋不同，它的运维是一种完全环境友好的方式。

segments.text: So, eco-housing like this is likely to become much more ...
segments.translation: 因此，这样的生态住宅有可能会变得更多……
